page: Microsoft Azure F32ads_v7 is a virtual machine instance from the Fadsv7 family, built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. This server features 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs, operating at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with 3780 GB of local disk capacity and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the F32ads_v7 demonstrates strong compute and memory capabilities. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng ranks in the top tier (top 10%) with a score of 3541.18, while multi-core performance is strong (top 25%) at 111314.11. The processor's instruction set extensions, including AVX-512 and cryptography flags like AES and SHA_NI, contribute to a top-tier single-threaded gzip compression speed of 38.0 GB/sec and a PassMark database operations score of 37385.95. Memory bandwidth is also robust, reaching 6116.97 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 214.06 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, both falling into the top 25%.

From a resource density perspective, the F32ads_v7 offers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio combined with a substantial 3780 GB local storage allocation, making it highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space without the overhead of external storage. However, the lack of hyperthreading (1.0 thread per core) and the absence of a GPU are notable tradeoffs for parallelized multi-threaded scaling and hardware-accelerated graphics or deep learning tasks. This instance is highly suited for database operations, web serving (achieving 56.98 GB/sec static throughput), medium-scale LLM inference (processing 185.65 tokens/sec on 7B models), and general-purpose compute workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure F32ads_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Fadsv7 family, powered by a dedicated 32-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core) and 3780 GB of local storage, with no bundled GPUs. The instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and database operations, alongside strong multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high-speed local storage, it offers excellent resource density for data-intensive applications. The lack of hyperthreading and GPU acceleration represents a tradeoff for highly parallelized or deep learning workloads. It is best suited for database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F32ads_v7 is a 32-vCPU virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor at 4.5 GHz, 128 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core and database benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F32ads_v7 features 32 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, optimized for high-performance database workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency dedicated processor instance featuring balanced memory allocation and extensive local storage for demanding database and compute workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated 32-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz.
Includes 128.0 GB of RAM and a large 3780 GB local storage capacity.
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and database operations benchmarks.
Strong memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput of 56.98 GB/sec.
Well-suited for database hosting, web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference.
